verdict_reasoning: SRC is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically resident in the cytosol and associated with the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. However, SRC has well-documented cancer-cell-restricted surface display and cell-state-induced ecto-kinase activity. Tumor cells show outer-leaflet SRC presentation, where it phosphorylates extracellular substrates — this is a bona-fide ecto-form distinct from the baseline intracellular pool. Additionally, SRC is involved in ligand-induced receptor clustering and signaling at ER-PM junctions, implicating a minority PM-cycling pool even in non-transformed cells. The cancer ecto-kinase phenotype and documented activation-induced PM trafficking qualify SRC as surface-accessible under non-baseline cellular states.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The extent and functional significance of baseline (non-cancer) PM-cycling vs. purely inner-leaflet-anchored cytoplasmic residence. Cancer-cell ecto-SRC is well-documented, but the magnitude of ecto-pool and its targeting potential in non-transformed cells remains incompletely characterized.
